mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show a positive correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market deterioration signals an impending economic contraction, causing investors to de-risk and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 70 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power during expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and stable consumer prices indicate robust consumer demand without inflationary pressure, boosting corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL rises above 3 percent year-over-year, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which immediately lifts equity prices over the next week during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction when tested against historical recession onsets.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product directly increases corporate profit margins and dividend payouts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index above 220.0 signals entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn positive in the majority of months following CPIAUCSL breaches above 220.0, or the threshold shows no predictive edge in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative returns when tested against historical data, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Surging housing starts indicate peak construction activity and late-cycle economic overheating, which historically precedes credit tightening and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST spikes consistently precede positive 60-day SPX returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal shows no predictive power when tested against housing market downturns.
